page_title: "kanidm_oauth2_basic Resource - kanidm"

---

# kanidm_oauth2_basic (Resource)

Manages a Kanidm OAuth2 basic (confidential) client.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Example: OAuth2 client for Grafana
resource "kanidm_oauth2_basic" "grafana" {
  name        = "grafana"
  displayname = "Grafana"
  origin      = "https://grafana.example.com"
  image_path  = "${path.module}/grafana.png"

  redirect_uris = [
    "https://grafana.example.com/login/generic_oauth"
  ]

  scope_map {
    group  = "admins"
    scopes = ["openid", "profile", "email", "groups"]
  }

  scope_map {
    group  = "developers"
    scopes = ["openid", "profile", "email"]
  }
}

# Store the client secret securely
output "grafana_client_secret" {
  description = "OAuth2 client secret for Grafana"
  value       = kanidm_oauth2_basic.grafana.client_secret
  sensitive   = true
}

# Example: OAuth2 client for Authentik
resource "kanidm_oauth2_basic" "authentik" {
  name        = "authentik"
  displayname = "Authentik SSO"
  origin      = "https://auth.example.com"

  redirect_uris = [
    "https://auth.example.com/source/oauth/callback/kanidm/"
  ]

  scope_map {
    group  = "all-users"
    scopes = ["openid", "profile", "email"]
  }
}

# Example: OAuth2 client for GitLab
resource "kanidm_oauth2_basic" "gitlab" {
  name        = "gitlab"
  displayname = "GitLab"
  origin      = "https://gitlab.example.com"

  redirect_uris = [
    "https://gitlab.example.com/users/auth/openid_connect/callback"
  ]

  scope_map {
    group  = "developers"
    scopes = ["openid", "profile", "email", "groups"]
  }

  scope_map {
    group  = "project-managers"
    scopes = ["openid", "profile", "email"]
  }
}

# Example: Simple OAuth2 client without scope maps
resource "kanidm_oauth2_basic" "simple_app" {
  name        = "simple-app"
  displayname = "Simple Application"
  origin      = "https://app.example.com"

  redirect_uris = [
    "https://app.example.com/callback"
  ]
}

# Example: Imported existing OAuth2 client
# Import command: terraform import kanidm_oauth2_basic.existing client_name
# Note: Client secret will be automatically retrieved from Kanidm after import
resource "kanidm_oauth2_basic" "existing" {
  name        = "existing-client"
  displayname = "Existing OAuth2 Client"
  origin      = "https://existing.example.com"
}

# The client secret is available even for imported clients
output "existing_client_secret" {
  description = "OAuth2 client secret for imported client"
  value       = kanidm_oauth2_basic.existing.client_secret
  sensitive   = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `displayname` (String) Display name of the OAuth2 client.
- `name` (String) OAuth2 client name (client ID).
- `origin` (String) Origin URL where the OAuth2 client application is hosted.

### Optional

- `allow_insecure_client_disable_pkce` (Boolean) Whether this confidential client may disable PKCE. Only use for legacy clients that cannot support PKCE.
- `claim_map` (Block Set) Claim mappings that define custom OIDC claims for members of specific groups. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--claim_map))
- `image_path` (String) Optional local path to an image file to upload.
- `jwt_legacy_crypto_enable` (Boolean) Whether Kanidm should enable legacy JWT crypto compatibility for this client.
- `prefer_short_username` (Boolean) Whether Kanidm should emit the bare username in the preferred_username claim instead of the full SPN.
- `redirect_uris` (Set of String) Set of allowed redirect URIs for OAuth2 callbacks.
- `scope_map` (Block Set) Scope mappings that define which OAuth2 scopes are granted to members of specific groups. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--scope_map))
- `sup_scope_map` (Block Set) Supplemental scope mappings that are automatically granted and cannot be declined. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--sup_scope_map))

### Read-Only

- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) Client secret for the OAuth2 basic client. Only available during creation.
- `id` (String) Stable Kanidm UUID for this OAuth2 client.
- `image_sha256` (String) SHA-256 hash of the local image file last applied.

<a id="nestedblock--claim_map"></a>
### Nested Schema for `claim_map`

Required:

- `group` (String) UUID of the Kanidm group to map claim values to.
- `join` (String) Join strategy for this claim name. Valid values: csv, ssv, array.
- `name` (String) Name of the custom claim.
- `values` (Set of String) Set of claim values to grant to group members.


<a id="nestedblock--scope_map"></a>
### Nested Schema for `scope_map`

Required:

- `group` (String) UUID of the Kanidm group to map scopes to.
- `scopes` (Set of String) Set of OAuth2 scopes to grant to group members.


<a id="nestedblock--sup_scope_map"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sup_scope_map`

Required:

- `group` (String) UUID of the Kanidm group to map scopes to.
- `scopes` (Set of String) Set of OAuth2 scopes to grant to group members.
